title: Apply for a lane rental scheme
body: "A lane rental scheme allows a local highway authority to charge works promoters for the time that street and road works occupy the highway.  What you need to complete the form  To complete the lane rental scheme application form, you will need to provide details of the scheme and the highway authority or authorities you’re applying for.  You will be asked to upload the following documents to support your application:   cover letter that confirms the application complies with legislative requirements, including a letterhead with the legal name of the authority signed by a chief executive, or similar completed scheme document providing the information requested in DfT’s bidding guidance, including proposed network coverage and charging policies completed environmental principles policy statement full list of proposed streets that lane rental will apply to and details of why each street is designated as traffic sensitive completed cost-benefit analysis form for the scheme evaluation plan for the proposed scheme summary of your local consultation that confirms this has taken place, outcome and responses to this consultation and details of where proposals were amended copies of your completed permit scheme evaluation reports   Read guidance on the above documents and download a template for the lane rental cost-benefit analysis and the environmental policy statement.  Start your application  Once you have all the documents you need, you can use this form to submit them. You can save your progress if you need to complete the form in stages.  Start now   Support  For any questions about your application, contact streetmanager@dft.gov.uk. "
